_doc: Pinned manifest for github.com/anthropics/financial-services SKILL.md files we adapted into .claude/skills/. Updated only by tooling_freshness_review after CFO sign-off. Parallel to memory/upstream_skills_pin.json (which tracks anthropics/skills patterns adapted into scripts/).

considered_upstream_skills.name: nav-tieout
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — fund-admin LP capital-account tie-out, not applicable to a SaaS finance team

considered_upstream_skills.name: xlsx-author
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — scripts/xlsx/ already covers headless authoring with claim_id provenance, blue/black/green coding, builders for BSR/MOR/parent FP&A report-out

considered_upstream_skills.name: pptx-author
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — scripts/pptx/ already covers headless authoring with claim_id speaker-notes stamping and the four primary deck builders

considered_upstream_skills.name: deck-refresh
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — our model is regenerate-from-claims (cleaner provenance), not in-place refresh of an existing deck

considered_upstream_skills.name: clean-data-xls
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — our ingest is deterministic from connectors, not interactive cleaning of user-provided sheets

considered_upstream_skills.name: ppt-template-creator
considered_upstream_skills.decision: deferred — meta-skill for creating template skills from a .pptx file; reconsider if a parent-branded template is shipped we need to wrap

considered_upstream_skills.name: 3-statement-model
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — equity-research/IB workflow, we don't model equities

considered_upstream_skills.name: competitive-analysis
considered_upstream_skills.decision: not relevant — IB/equity-research framing; our competitive context is captured in CLAUDE.md §3 and in variance-commentary-structure

audit-xls.notes: Adapted: dropped IB/DCF/LBO model-type-specific bug lists; kept BS-balance / cash-tieout / formula-error / hardcode-detection / roll-forward integrity checks; tied severity to materiality.yaml; output integrates with review/findings.json. Scope locked to outputs/reporting/artifacts/close_pack.xlsx.

accrual-schedule.notes: Adapted: policy list inputs from memory/accrual_policy.yaml; engine-MSA-bundled accruals get explicit open_question per CLAUDE.md §4; emits per-accrual claims; draft-only, never posts. Self-check 7 (accruals_consistent_with_policy) returns n_a until policy is populated.

break-trace.notes: Adapted: buckets are GL-internal (intercompany, sum-of-entities, parent-chart, FX-rate-source) plus subledger when gl-recon is live; owner enum extended with parent-chart and escalate-cfo; defers to parent-chart-reconciliation and deferred-rev-rollforward where applicable.

gl-recon.notes: Adapted for the SaaS subledger feed (forthcoming): keyset is customer_id+product_line+posting_period (or tail_number+transaction_id for records-transfer transactions); SaaS-specific buckets (recognition-period-break) and causes (ssp_allocation_drift, long_term_services_bundle_alloc, prepay_recognition) added. Skill on the shelf until subledger connector lands; tolerance field defaults null in materiality.yaml.

audit-pptx.notes: Adapted from ib-check-deck; dropped IB-specific language polish (we have writing-style for voice). Kept four QC dimensions: number consistency across slides, claim_id-in-speaker-notes verification, narrative-data alignment, source citations + formatting hygiene. Scope: MOR / BSR / parent-FP&A-reportout decks. Reuses scripts/pptx/templates.py speaker-notes claim_id stamping pattern.
